message:
🐛 Fix brew relink: rm + unlink before link for proper recovery (#2554)

brew link --overwrite doesn't work when the broken file is a regular
file (not a symlink) — brew's internal state still tracks the formula
as linked. The correct sequence is:
1. rm the 0-byte file
2. brew unlink (clears brew's internal linked state)
3. brew link (creates proper symlink)

Tested by simulating the broken state and verifying full recovery.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -298,7 +298,9 @@ else
         BREW_BIN="$(command -v kc-agent 2>/dev/null || true)"
         if [ -n "$BREW_BIN" ] && [ ! -s "$BREW_BIN" ]; then
             echo -e "${YELLOW}Detected broken kc-agent binary (0 bytes), relinking...${NC}"
-            brew link --overwrite kc-agent 2>/dev/null || true
+            rm -f "$BREW_BIN"
+            brew unlink kc-agent 2>/dev/null || true
+            brew link kc-agent 2>/dev/null || true
             BREW_BIN="$(command -v kc-agent 2>/dev/null || true)"
         fi
